data_description.device_profile: Select the matching device profile for your Zendure system. The profile contains tuned control parameters.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent. This is the central basis for all decisions.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional sensor from the Zendure integration that reports active BMS limits (e.g. charge or discharge lock).
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. Multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate the total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the AC power of the battery. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI will block discharging.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh (e.g. from the Zendure integration). If set, it overrides the manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value will be used for relative summer/winter thresholds and other PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts. Used to detect surplus.
data_description.price_now_entity: Sensor with the current electricity price (e.g. Tibber). Used for economic decisions.
data_description.price_export_entity: Sensor with price forecast data (e.g. Tibber / EPEX). Enables price-based planning.
data_description.grid_mode: Choose how your grid consumption is measured: one combined sensor or separate sensors for import and export.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during grid import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power (positive value only).
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power (positive value only).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Select entity from the Zendure integration that switches between charging (INPUT) and discharging (OUTPUT).
data_description.input_limit_entity: Number entity used to set the charging power in watts.
data_description.output_limit_entity: Number entity used to set the discharging power in watts.

reconfigure_grid.title: Configure grid measurement
reconfigure_grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

grid.title: Configure grid measurement
grid.description: Select the grid sensors matching the mode

data_description.grid_power_entity: A single sensor with positive power during import and negative power during export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Sensor with current grid import power in watts.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor with current grid export power in watts.

data_description.device_profile: Select the matching device profile for your Zendure system. Control parameters are adjusted automatically.
data_description.soc_entity: Sensor with the current battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.soc_limit_entity: Optional status sensor from the Zendure integration for detecting active BMS locks.
data_description.pack_capacity_kwh: Usable capacity of a single battery pack in kWh. Multiplied by the number of installed packs to calculate the total capacity.
data_description.battery_ac_power_entity: Sensor for the AC power of the battery. The value must be positive during discharge. Recommended: sensor.solarflow_2400_ac_bat_in_out
data_description.additional_battery_charge_entity: Optional sensor of a second battery system. If this sensor reports positive charging power, Battery SmartFlow AI will block discharging.
data_description.capacity_entity: Optional sensor with the automatically calculated available battery capacity in kWh (e.g. from the Zendure integration). If set, it overrides the manual calculation.
data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value will be used for relative summer/winter thresholds and other PV-related logic.
data_description.pv_entity: Sensor with the current PV power in watts.
data_description.price_now_entity: Current electricity price. Enables price-based discharging.
data_description.price_export_entity: Price forecast data for the coming hours. Enables intelligent charging planning.
data_description.grid_mode: Defines how grid import and export are measured.
data_description.grid_power_entity: A combined grid sensor (positive = import, negative = export).
data_description.ac_mode_entity: Zendure AC mode (INPUT/OUTPUT). Controlled automatically by the integration.

init.title: Edit control profile
init.description: Choose the section you want to edit. “General” contains shared profile parameters, “Charge” the control values for charging power, and “Discharge” the control values for discharging power.

general.title: Edit control profile – General
general.description: General control parameters of the active device profile.

data_description.installed_pv_wp: Installed theoretical PV module power of the system in Wp. This value is used for relative summer/winter thresholds and other PV-related logic.
data_description.TARGET_IMPORT_W: Target value for a small intentional grid import. A higher value reduces the risk of unwanted export but slightly increases grid consumption.
data_description.EXPORT_GUARD_W: Additional protection margin against unwanted export. Higher values make the control more conservative.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_DEFICIT_W: Minimum deficit at which discharging is kept active. Helps prevent oscillation at very small loads.
data_description.KEEPALIVE_MIN_OUTPUT_W: Minimum discharge power to keep discharging stably active. Prevents unnecessary switching on and off.
data_description.SOC_DISCHARGE_RESUME_MARGIN: Additional SoC distance above the configured minimum SoC that must be reached before discharging is re-enabled after a SoC lock. Prevents oscillation between charging and discharging at low battery level, especially in the morning with fluctuating PV power.

charge.title: Edit control profile – Charge
charge.description: Control parameters for the charging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.CHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for charge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes charging smoother, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing charging power. Higher values raise charging power faster, but can lead to more unstable behavior.
data_description.CHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when reducing charging power. Higher values reduce charging power faster, but can cause larger jumps.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing charging power. Higher values make charge control faster, but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.CHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing charging power. Higher values reduce charging faster, but can make behavior less smooth.

discharge.title: Edit control profile – Discharge
discharge.description: Control parameters for the discharging behavior of the active device profile.

data_description.DISCHARGE_DEADBAND_W: Tolerance range for discharge control in which no adjustment is made. A higher value makes discharging smoother, a lower value reacts faster and more precisely.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_UP: Gain when increasing discharge power. Higher values raise discharge power faster, but can overshoot more easily.
data_description.DISCHARGE_KP_DOWN: Gain when reducing discharge power. Higher values reduce discharge power faster, but can lead to more unstable behavior.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_UP: Maximum power step per control cycle when increasing discharge power. Higher values make discharge control faster, but potentially more aggressive.
data_description.DISCHARGE_MAX_STEP_DOWN: Maximum power step per control cycle when reducing discharge power. Higher values reduce discharge faster, but can cause larger jumps.

state.expensive_discharge: Discharging because of high electricity price
state.very_expensive_force_discharge: Forced discharge because of extreme price spike
state.soc_min_resume_block: Discharging blocked (re-enable threshold not yet reached)
